Investigation of mass, charge and energy of
.Thermal neutrons. The incoming flux was about 5.E+9
The Cosi-Fan-Tutte spectrometer was used. The
fragments were identified by a simultaneous
determination of time of flight, pulse height in an
ionization chamber and the Bragg-curve parameter Q.
The flight-path length (107.2+-0.1 cm) was calibrated
with the 6Li(n,a)t reaction products produced in a 6Li
sample and 5.554 MeV a-line originating from the decay
Run with a 350 microg/cm2 evaporated 235UF(4) sample
was performed as test of spectrometer and method of
data analysis - excellent agreement for 235U(nth,f) of
our results with the Lohengrin data of W.Lang+.
Ionization chamber at the end of the flight
path. Two pick-up detectors were used for
.A 7 microgram per square cm Pu-241 acetate sample.
electrosprayed onto a 30 microgram per cm2 polyamide
plutonium isotopes (mainly Pu-241) was less than 1%
.The kinetic energies were corrected for energy loss
in the sample and the start detector.
The thermal-fission contribution of the other plutonium
Data all refer to mass, charge and kinetic-energy

energy is a direct preneutron characteristic of the
